title: Deep Blue World
subtitle: A First Look at Ocean Animals
author: Hannah Whitfield
subject: ocean animals and the watery places they live
flux_style: Soft hand-painted watercolour storybook illustration in an adorable, sweet kawaii style — large friendly sparkly eyes, a gentle happy face, soft edges and tender huggable charm — with every animal drawn in its OWN correct natural body shape and true proportions for its real species (flat animals stay flat, long animals stay long, never forced into a uniform round chubby ball), loose simplified naive childlike style, cool ocean palette of blues aquas and teal with sunlit water, gentle visible brushwork, soft edges, painterly flat shapes, bright friendly, children's picture book art, not photorealistic, not a photograph, no text
topics: a big friendly blue whale gliding through deep blue ocean water
a pod of playful dolphins leaping over sparkling blue waves, the painted sky and rolling sea filling the whole frame edge to edge
a green sea turtle swimming over a sunlit coral reef
a fluffy harbor seal pup resting on a smooth ocean rock
a family of penguins on the ice beside the cold blue sea
a little orange clownfish peeking out of a large swaying sea anemone, with a colourful coral reef filling the whole underwater scene
a bright blue tang fish gliding through clear sunlit blue water
a friendly shark with a tall dorsal fin and a large upright vertical tail fin (not a horizontal dolphin fluke) cruising through clear blue water
a friendly swordfish with a long pointed sword-like bill gliding swiftly through the deep open blue ocean
a sea otter floating on its back in calm blue water
an orca swimming through deep blue ocean water
a narwhal with its long tusk under the cold arctic sea
a round pufferfish among soft green seaweed
a walrus resting on the ice at the edge of the sea
a glowing jellyfish drifting through deep blue water
a friendly sea lion resting on a smooth rock beside the calm blue sea
a curious octopus with exactly eight curling tentacle arms among the rocks on the ocean floor
a bright striped angelfish with a tall flat body and flowing fins gliding through a sunlit coral reef

a gentle grey manatee with ONE single solid rounded spatula-shaped paddle tail like a beaver's tail or a ping-pong paddle (one continuous un-split fin, absolutely NOT a forked, notched, two-lobed or whale-fluke tail) floating by green seagrass in calm shallow water
a classic five-pointed starfish with exactly five arms resting in a sparkling tide pool
art_prompt: a soft watercolour storybook front cover scene set fully underwater in deep sunlit blue ocean, an adorable cute baby blue whale with a big round smooth head, large friendly sparkly eyes and rosy cheeks, and two cute playful dolphins swimming together, a sweet little green sea turtle and small colourful fish nearby, exaggerated kawaii cuteness, smooth rounded whale and dolphin heads, no water spout, no blowhole spray, no antennae, no twigs or sprigs growing from any head, soft even gentle underwater light spread evenly across the whole scene, no strong light beams, no bright glare, no single concentrated bright spot or glowing pool, evenly and brightly lit richly painted deep-blue water, bright cheerful and inviting, cool saturated ocean blues and aquas balanced evenly left to right, no text, no words
blurb: Dive into a deep blue world! From a gentle blue whale gliding through the open sea to a tiny starfish resting in a sparkling tide pool, this gentle first ocean book takes early readers on a tour of twenty sea animals and the watery places they call home. Soft, glowing watercolours and simple, read-aloud rhymes make every page a warm discovery — a perfect first ocean adventure for curious little ones just beginning to read.
